channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: GungHo Online Entertainment announces Anime Expo 2026 plans
item.content:encoded:
Go hands-on with Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet.

GungHo Online Entertainment has announced its plans for Anime Expo 2026, which will run from July 2 to 5 at Los Angeles Convention Center.

Attendees will be among the first to go hands-on with the newly announced Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet. The booth will also feature a prize giveaway and various merchandise.

Here are the details:

Explore The Uncharted Planet at Anime Expo

Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet evolves the original’s fast-paced player-versus-player into a cooperative open-world action game. Players can explore a vast, nature-rich world where the series’ signature high-speed Ninja Gum Action drives every encounter. In this new world, combat now goes deeper than ever, with weapon attributes, item crafting, and a cooking system fueling quests you can tackle in any order. Brave them solo or team up with as many as four friends to take down dungeons and colossal bosses together.

The Anime Expo demo features two playable courses: a Planetary Exploration Course showcasing the game’s combat, and a Photography Course that lets players go on a sightseeing adventure across the world. Everyone who plays the demo will receive an inflatable bat modeled after the game’s IPPON Katana.

Chew Your Way to a Prize

Attendees can enter GungHo’s booth giveaway by following GungHo on social media and/or completing select tasks at the booth. Prizes include:

  • A Nintendo Switch 2 console
  • A Nintendo Switch 2 Pro Controller and merchandise bundle, with the winner’s choice of one game: Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet, Trails in the Sky 1st Chapter, LUNAR Remastered Collection, or GRANDIA HD Collection.
  • Nintendo eShop gift cards
  • Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet button pins with nine available designs

Gear Up, Shinobi!

A selection of merchandise will be available for purchase at booth #2316 across three of GungHo’s titles:

  • Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet
    • T-shirts ($45) – Available in black or white
    • Towels ($15)
    • Character acrylic stands ($20)
  • Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter
    • Mousepads ($15)
    • Phone charms ($15) – Two designs available
    • Tote bags ($30)
    • Buttons ($5) – Four designs available
    • Mugs ($25)
  • Puzzle & Dragons
    • Acrylic keychains ($15), stickers ($4 to $5), and clear files ($8) featuring designs from Events such as Halloween and PAD Island.
    • Puzzle & Dragons fans can stop by the booth to receive special in-game rewards—Scarlet Soaring Sword Suzaku, Leilan; Spectral Howling Fist Seiryuu, Karin; and Demonic Tearing Claw Byakko, Haku, plus the MAX + Limit Breakthrough Dungeon, all redeemed on-site via QR code and received at a later date.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/gungho-online-entertainment-announces-anime-expo-2026-plans
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tears of Metal launches in Early Access for PC on July 22
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Medieval hack-and-slash cooperative roguelike game dated.

Medieval hack-and-slash cooperative roguelike game Tears of Metal will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on July 22, developer Paper Cult Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

What to Expect at Launch

The Early Access version includes the complete core experience:

  • The full progression loop from beginning to end.
  • Three Acts, each with unique enemies, Elites, and Bosses.
  • Three playable Heroes.
  • Village upgrades, Emblems, Equipment, Companions, and more.

The game is fully playable from A to Z, but we believe Tears of Metal can become even better with community feedback. Early Access will allow us to continue expanding the game while working closely with players to improve and refine the experience.

During Early Access, you can expect:

  • New playable Heroes.
  • New Emblem Family.
  • New Equipment and Charms.
  • New Companions.
  • Additional Village content.
  • Balance updates and gameplay improvements.
  • Performance optimizations.
  • Quality-of-life features based on community feedback.

Demo Ending July 6

One final note: the demo will be removed on July 6.

The demo no longer reflects the current state of the game. Because it is significantly out of date, and because save files will not carry over to the full version, we’ve decided to retire it ahead of launch.

If you haven’t played the demo yet, this is your last chance to give it a try before it disappears.

Watch a new trailer below.

Early Access Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/tears-of-metal-launches-in-early-access-for-pc-on-july-22
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
